learning.summary: Image deployment installs a standardized operating system image onto one or more computers. It enables organizations to deploy identical operating systems, applications, and configurations quickly and consistently.
learning.explanation:
Image deployment begins with a reference (golden) system that contains the desired operating system, updates, drivers, applications, and configuration settings. An image of that system is captured and deployed to other computers.

This approach reduces installation time and ensures consistent configurations across an organization. Enterprise imaging solutions such as Microsoft Deployment Toolkit (MDT), Microsoft Configuration Manager, or similar tools are commonly used to automate and manage image deployments.

facts.text: A golden image is a standardized reference installation used to deploy multiple computers.
Image deployment installs the same operating system, applications, and configuration on multiple systems.
Image deployment is commonly used in enterprise environments.

examples.text: An organization deploys a standard Windows image with required software to every new employee laptop.

examTips.text: Know the purpose of a golden image and when image deployment is appropriate.

commonMistakes.text: Assuming every deployed computer must be configured manually after imaging.

scenarios.situation: Recommend image deployment for rolling out identical systems to many employees.
scenarios.expectedAction: Identify the correct concept or response.

pbqIdeas.task: Select the appropriate deployment method for an enterprise rollout.

reviewNotes: Golden image terminology and enterprise imaging tool examples are curriculum enrichment beyond the explicit transcript evidence.
